observations.recommendation:
1.15. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Prepare the annual Schedule of the delinquent stallholders and lot lessees as at
yearend and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant; and,
b) Prepare Demand Letters indicating the total unpaid monthly rentals and the
penalties thereof to all delinquent stallholders.

1.16. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of
accounts the receivables on the rentals from the Market Stall and the
Government Lots, that are already due for collections, based on the report
forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer and the corresponding income earned
therefrom.

2.17 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Update the Taxpayer’s Index Card and reconcile the same with the
records of the Municipal Assessor;
b) Submit the Taxpayer’s Index Card to the Municipal Accountant at the
start of each year as the basis in recognition of the RPT Receivables; and
c) Update the Integrated Taxation (iTax) Management System.

2.18 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record every January
of each year the full amount of RPT and SET due and collectible as receivables,
based on the computed amount per RPT Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s
index cards, and record the appropriate adjusting entries to report the actual
amounts of unpaid RPT and SET Receivables to arrive at an accurate and
reliable balance as at yearend.

2.19 During the Exit Conference, the Municipal Treasurer commented that it might take
them one to two years to complete the updating of the Taxpayer’s Index Card due
to the volume of index cards being maintained by the MTO. Nevertheless, he
committed to comply with the recommendations.

2.20 The Municipal Accountant also agreed to the recommendations.

3.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned
continuously adhere to the provisions of the aforementioned DBM-DOF-DILG
JMC, to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality
are achieved and the intended purposes of the 20% Development Fund are
realized.

3.11 During the Exit Conference, the Municipal Mayor gave her commitment to
continuously adhere to the aforecited provisions on the implementation of the
PPAs under the 20% Development Fund.

4.17 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor scrutinize the Memorandum of
Agreement entered into with any Funding Agency, especially those funds that
were charged under the 20% Development Fund and its prohibitions.

4.18 We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) scrutinize the Program of
Work before signing for recommending approval.

4.19 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel
concerned refrain from releasing funds from 20% Development Fund for
Barangays’ financial assistance that has expenditure items prohibited under
Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DF-DILG JMC No. 1.

5.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor monitor the complete
implementation of all programs, projects and activities funded by the National
Agencies, as stated and agreed in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

5.15 We recommended that the implementing Department Heads:
a) Retrieve all MOA or any related documents to determine the specific
purpose of each fund received from the National Agencies;
b) Implement the programs, projects and activities for the intended
purpose within the timelines agreed;
c) Request for counterpart fund as required in the MOA for the
implementation thereof; and,
d) Initiate a written formal request with the Source Agencies to allow the
LGU to further utilize the fund, especially those Agencies with Demand
Letters.

5.16 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Exhaust all efforts and remedies to determine the specific details on the
balances of the Due to NGA account; and,
b) Maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for each fund received from the National
Agencies.

5.17 We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Retain a copy of the MOA on all funds received from any Source
Agencies;
b) Return all unexpended balance or excess funds to the source agency;
and,
c) Return all idle funds with the Bureau of Treasury for those funds with
undeterminable Source Agency and/or its specific purpose, only after all
efforts and remedies are exhausted.

5.18 During the Exit Conference, the Municipal Mayor assured the Audit Team that the
recommendations will be promptly complied with.

5.19 The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer also committed to comply with
the recommendations.

6.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Thru the authority from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, execute a
Contract of Lease with the Stallholders of the Public Market;
b) Thru the members of the Market Committee, establish an effective and
efficient measures to increase the collections from the market stalls,
especially for those delinquent stallholders, such as, measures on valid
moratorium of penalties for a certain period or prohibiting the violators
from engaging business with the LGU, if warranted; and
c) Thru the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Treasurer, install a visible
identification number to each section of the Public Market.

6.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Immediately collect the rentals from all stallholders, including, the
imposition of the penalties of the delinquent stallholders;
b) Strictly monitor the payments of the monthly rental of each stallholder;
c) Issue Demand Letters to delinquent stallholders and enforce the closure
of the stall if they failed to immediately settle their obligations especially
those with delinquencies for three (3) consecutive months;
d) Investigate on apparent subleasing of market stalls and enforce penalties
thereon based on the provisions in the existing Revenue Code; and
e) Declare vacant to stalls whose tenants discontinue to operate their
business, and accordingly invite (thru public bidding) interested
applicants in accordance with Section 5A.08 of the Revenue Code.

7.15 We recommended that the Collecting Officers:
a) Submit to the Audit Team a written explanation on the delayed
remittance of collections to the Municipal Treasurer; and
b) Henceforth, remit all collections at the close of each day to the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that government funds are safeguarded from
possible loss through misuse or misappropriation.

7.16 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Diligently review and verify the RCDs submitted by the Collecting
Officers and meticulously validate the accountable forms for any
unremitted collections;
b) For control purposes, refrain the issuance/release of another unused
accountable forms to the Collecting Officers, unless collections issued
with previous accountable forms are fully remitted; and
c) Prepare the consolidated RCDs daily and transmit the same and its
supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant, every 5th day of the
ensuing month.

8.18 We recommended that the LDRRM Officer, OIC Municipal Budget Officer,
MDRRM Council and the Sangguniang Bayan submit written justifications on
why the revised Annual Investment Program and duly enacted Supplemental
Budget was not undertaken despite the changes made in the LDRRMF
Investment Plan.

8.19 Henceforth, we recommended that the OIC Municipal Budget Officer ensure
strict compliance with the budgeting process, particularly:
a) The specific projects and activities in the LDRRMF Investment Plan
should be integrated in the Annual Investment Program; and,
b) Changes in the projects and activities to be funded out of the LDRRM
Fund should be supported with a revised LDRRMF Investment Plan,
revised Annual Investment Program and covered by a Supplemental
Budget pursuant to R.A. 7160 and its IRR.

8.20 During the Exit Conference, the Officials concerned agreed to the
recommendations.

8.21 Also, the Budget Officer committed to strictly comply with the budgeting
process.

9.25 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer record all donations-in-
kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment
Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not
necessarily in the required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining
undistributed items, if any; and submit a copy of the same to the Supply
Officer and the Municipal Accountant

9.26 We also recommended that the Supply Officer, assist the Municipal Health
Officer in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-kind, and in the
conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any.

9.27 We further recommended that the Supply Officer maintain copies of the
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and its
supporting documents and prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief
Goods for DRRM.

9.28 Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, based on the
Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, make a disclosure in the Notes to
FS as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind
received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines
and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

9.29 During the Exit Conference, the Municipal Health Officer submitted his reply to
AOM No. 2023-04, stating, among others, that they were not informed of the need
to prepare the required documentations as discussed above. Nevertheless, he
assured the Audit Team that the recommendation will be complied with.

9.30 On behalf of the Supply Officer, who was unable to attend the Exit Conference, the
Municipal Treasurer committed that the recommendations will be complied with.

9.31 The Municipal Accountant also committed to make a disclosure in the Notes to FS
on the donated relief goods.

10.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the accountable officers, especially those with long overdue cash
advances, and those outgoing elected officials and employees before
issuing their certificates of clearance, to immediately settle or refund
their unliquidated cash advance, otherwise, cause the withholding of his
salary pursuant to 2nd Paragraph of Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-
002 dated February 10, 1997;
b) Ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and
c) Initiate the request for write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances,
thru the Municipal Accountant, especially to those accountable officers
who have already died and/or absconded, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, only after exhausting
all possible remedies.

10.16 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare individual
subsidiary ledgers for each Accountable Officer with proper disclosure or
classification whether they are still employed, have resigned or deceased, in
order for the Municipality to properly plan the courses of action to be made
in the settlement of the cash advances.

10.18 During the Exit Conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to the
recommendations.

11.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Budget Officer submit to the Audit Team a justification on the
grant of Telephone Mobile Subsistence to a non-Department Head.

11.16 We also recommended for the submission of the actual usage of the telephone
mobile subsistence expenses. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a
Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.

12.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal HRMO
undertake the following measures:
a) Ensure that employment of Contract of Service is aligned with Republic
Act No. 9184;
b) Ensure that employment of Job Order workers uses the pro-forma
agreement as required under Section 9 of the CSC Resolution No. 021430
dated November 12, 2002;
c) Execute an agreement for COS or JO workers without alteration or
passage of items in hand written, particularly the names of the hired
personnel, contract period and the amount of daily wages; and,
d) Indicate the complete name of the COS or JO workers, which includes
the middle name or initial thereof, to avoid confusion or duplication in
the payroll.

12.17 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the completeness
and propriety of the supporting documents for the payment of COS or JO
workers.

12.18 We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that payment
of COS or JO workers is made within the allowable amount and within the
contract period as indicated or as appearing in the agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: 1. Create an Inventory Committee, who will be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less; 2. Supervise, thru the Committee, the preparation of the Physical Inventory Plan and the submission of a copy to the COA Audit Team, at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and 3. Supervise the conduct of the physical count and the immediate preparation of a Report on Physical Count of PPE using the prescribed form and the submission of the report to the Audit Team, and 4. Initiate the implementation of all prior years’ audit recommendations on PPE account, as well as the full implementation of the “one-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided in the COA Circular 2020-006. 5. Consider the appointment of a General Services Officer (GSO) who shall take charge of property management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to correct the Infrastructure Assets and the corresponding accumulated depreciation.

To put the accounts in order, we recommended: 7. The Municipal Treasurer summarize all the Requisition and Issue Vouchers received during the year in a Report of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit this to the Municipal Accountant. 8. The Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the issued/distributed/consumed inventories based on the Report of the Municipal Treasurer. Moving forward, we recommended 9. The Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories every end of the semester, prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories and submit to the Audit Team every July and January of the year. 10. The Municipal Treasurer, who is also the Property Officer record all purchases in Stock Cards; issue inventories based on approved RIS; consolidate all the RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued every end of the month and submit this to the Municipal Accountant. 11. We further recommended that the concerned personnel in-charge on the distribution of relief goods and other supplies and materials for distribution to submit every end of the month the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) with the supporting Requisition and Issue Slips and Distribution Lists to the accounting unit which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the issuances during the month.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Implemented

Implemented
Implemented

observations.recommendation: 12. Prepare the adjusting entry to close the Accounts Payable totaling ₱1,311,069.20, as soon as it is ascertained that there are no valid claims; 13. Henceforth, record as Accounts Payable only those obligated expenditures with valid claims; and 14. Retain the original file of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents recorded as Accounts Payable until its payment.

15. Transfer the funds deposited in the SEF and TF to the GF bank account; and 16. Send written notice to the payee after it is determined that the stale check is eligible for replacement. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 17. Determine that cause of the unaccounted discrepancies between the Trust Liability account and actual balance of cash in bank, including the unaccounted reconciling items; and 18. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries on all identified reconciling items.

19. Inform all Department Heads on the cut-off date in the submission of all claims before the year-end, for inclusion as Accountable Payable, and strictly impose the same, otherwise, decline the processing for those claims that were submitted beyond the cut-off date; 20. Record as Accounts Payable only those with valid claims on the year where the related expenses were incurred; and 21. Monitor the payments of accounts payables, especially those accounts payable that remained unclaimed as at year-end.

22. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) observe the required period of each procurement activities and the allowable period of completion. 23. We also recommended that the BAC prepare the semestral report on Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR), including the monitoring on the schedule of each procurement activity.

24. We recommended that the BAC and TWG conduct a Pre-Procurement Conference prior to the issuance of the Invitation to BID to the prospective bidders to ensure that the procurement is made in accordance with the procurement law. assess the readiness of the procurement undertaken.

25. We recommended that the BAC and TWG to observe the proper conduct of post-qualification activity and to submit the Post-Qualification Report together with other supporting documents to prove that they verify and validate the documents submitted by the Bidder with Lowest Calculated Bid.

26. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor revise the MOA with the Municipal barangay to include the provisions on the specific project to be implemented and the period within which the project is to be implemented; 27. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor supervise the full implementation of any programs, projects and activities funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline and agreements provided in the MOA. 28. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Issue demand letters to the barangays concerned for the immediate submission of liquidation reports and to record the same on the month these are submitted; and Attach to disbursement voucher the certification that the previous financial assistance was already liquidated and accounted in books of accounts.

29. We recommended that the Municipal DRRM Office maximize the utilization of the Local DRRM Fund by implementing all the programs, projects and activities in the LDRRMF Investment Plan. 30. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA Auditor. 31. We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare and regularly update separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for (a.) QRF, (b.) MOOE and (c.) Capital Outlay for LDRRM Fund.

32. Include in the LDRRMIP the program on Stockpiling of Basic Emergency supplies such as food packs as priorities in the PPAs. 33. We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor and LDRRM Council immediately provide emergency assistance to victims of calamity or and disaster in order to save lives, reduce health impacts, ensure public safety and meet the basic subsistence needs of the people effected.

34. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through its Municipal Development Council to include in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) the projects which are implementation ready to expedite the prosecution of the projects. 35. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer to implement all identified projects within the year to ensure the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

36. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor revise the MOA with the barangay to include the provisions on the specific project to be implemented and the period within which the project is to be implemented; 37. We also recommended that the MPDC should specifically identify the projects, in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) on the financial assistance to the barangays. Otherwise, if generic term was used, a separate authority from the Sangguniang Bayan is necessary to authorize the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract for each financial assistance. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant; 38. Issue demand letters to the barangays concerned for the immediate submission of liquidation reports and to record the same on the month these are submitted; and 39. Attach to disbursement voucher the certification that the previous financial assistance was already liquidated and accounted in books of accounts.

40. draft the Contract of Lease for each lessee, specifying all necessary provisions for compliance by both parties; 41. submit the Contract of Lease duly signed by the Municipal Mayor, the lessees, together with the master list; 42. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor convene its Local Finance Committee and require them to review the current Revenue Code of the Municipality, verify if rental of lots were included therein and recommend revisions to update the rates and fees, for submission to the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this report. The SB Council is now formulating an Ordinance, including the review of the Revenue Code and the assignment of responsibilities. The BAC and other entities to follow procedures.

observations.recommendation: 43. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain separate books of accounts for the Municipality’s water system operations and record therein the monthly billings, collections and the related disbursements. 44. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the documents necessary to open a separate bank account for the LGU-Caibiran water system operations.
45. pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by the RTWPB for Region VIII and consider reduction of the number of JOs in the LGU to those which are considered essential and urgent
46. pay the mandated refund of the amount from all persons liable thereto.
observations.managementAction: They were initiated to refund the amount thru employee’s salary deduction, with their Affidavit of Undertakings, thus, the amount was not yet fully refunded.

observations.recommendation: 47. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Head of HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documentary requirements provided under COA Circular 2012-001 including the proof that conditions cited under DBM Budget Circular No. 2017-3 were complied with.
observations.managementAction: Not all required reports were provided

observations.recommendation: 48. Impose penal sanctions provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 to these employees; Ensure strict compliance and initiate the request for write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances, thru the Municipal Accountant, especially to those accountable officers who have already died and/or absconded, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, only after exhausting all possible remedies. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare individual subsidiary ledgers for each Accountable Officer with proper disclosure or classification whether they are still employed, have resigned or deceased, in order for the Municipality to properly plan the courses of action to be made in the settlement of the cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Difficulty in settling the unliquidated cash advance specially those employees who are no longer connected with LGU.

observations.recommendation: 49. We recommended that the BAC should prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and submit the approved PMR to the Government Procurement Policy Board in the prescribed format as required by law.

50. We recommended to require the concerned officials or its equivalent to strictly observe the provisions of Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Not all required documentary requirements were submitted

observations.recommendation: 51. We recommended that the Management attach a legal document supporting the payment of such additional bonus benefits.
observations.managementAction: For verification of the submitted documents on the legal basis of additional bonus payments.

observations.recommendation:
1.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a) create an Inventory Committee, who will be temporarily relieved of all
their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months
or less;
b) supervise, thru the Committee, the preparation of the Physical Inventory
Plan and the submission of a copy to the COA Audit Team, at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and
c) supervise the conduct of the physical count and the immediate preparation
of a Report on Physical Count of PPE using the prescribed form and the
submission of the report to the Audit Team, and
d) initiate the implementation of all prior years’ audit recommendations on PPE
account, as well as the full implementation of the “one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts” as provided in the COA Circular 2020-006.

1.14. Consider the appointment of a General Services Officer (GSO) who shall take
charge of property management.

2.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries
to correct the Infrastructure Assets and the corresponding accumulated
depreciation.

2.9. The Municipal Accountant promised to prepare the required adjusting entries and
the correct computation of the depreciation of the assets.

3.10. To put the accounts in order, we recommended:
a) The Municipal Treasurer summarize all the Requisition and Issue
Vouchers received during the year in a Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued and submit this to the Municipal Accountant.
b) The Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the
issued/distributed/consumed inventories based on the Report of the
Municipal Treasurer.

3.11. Moving forward, we recommended:
a) The Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct a
physical count of inventories every end of the semester, prepare the Report
on Physical Count of Inventories and submit to the Audit Team every July
and January of the year.
b) The Municipal Treasurer, who is also the Property Officer record all
purchases in Stock Cards; issue inventories based on approved RIS;
consolidate all the RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
every end of the month and submit this to the Municipal Accountant.

3.12. The Management is amenable with the recommendations.

4.11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the adjusting entry to close the Accounts Payable totaling
₱1,311,069.20, as soon as it is ascertained that there are no valid claims;
b) henceforth, record as Accounts Payable only those obligated expenditures
with valid claims; and
c) retain the original file of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting
documents recorded as Accounts Payable until its payment.

4.12. The Municipal Accountant promised to prepare the required adjusting entries and
to adopt the recommendations.

5.23. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
(a) transfer the funds erroneously deposited in the SEF and TF to the GF
bank account; and
(b) send written notice to the payee after it is determined that the stale check
is eligible for replacement.

5.24. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
(a) determine the cause of the unaccounted discrepancies between the Trust
Liability account and actual balance of cash in bank, including the
unaccounted reconciling items; and
(b) prepare the necessary adjusting entries on all identified reconciling items.

5.25. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant made an assurance to
implement the recommendations.

6.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) inform all Department Heads on the cut-off date in the submission of all
claims before the year-end, for inclusion as Accounts Payable, and
strictly impose of the same, otherwise, decline the processing for those
claims that were submitted beyond the cut-off date;
b) record as Accounts Payable only those with valid claims on the year
where the related expenses were incurred; and
c) monitor the payments of accounts payables, especially those accounts
payable that remained unclaimed as at year-end.

6.15. The Municipal Accountant is amenable with the recommendations.

7.13. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) observe the
required period of each procurement activities and the allowable period of
completion.

7.14. We also recommended that the BAC prepare the semestral report on
Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR), including the monitoring on the
schedule of each procurement activity.

7.15. The BAC promised to observe the required period of procurement activities and the
preparation of the required monitoring report.

8.9. We recommended that the BAC and TWG conduct a Pre-Procurement
Conference prior to the issuance of the Invitation to Bid to ensure that the
procurement is made in accordance with the procurement law.

8.10. The BAC submitted the required Minutes on the conduct of Pre-Procurement
activities and promised to faithfully attach the said report together with the
submission of other bidding documents.

9.13. We recommended that the BAC and TWG to observe the proper conduct of
post-qualification activity and to submit the Post-Qualification Report
together with other supporting documents to prove that they verify and
validate the documents submitted by the Bidder with Lowest Calculated Bid.

9.14. The BAC explained that a post-qualification was conducted in the bidding process,
only that they were not able to prepare the required documents. But they made an
assurance to prepare the required reports.

10.19. We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture, in coordination with the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, return the unutilized fund
from YRRP to the Department of Agriculture, including the submission of the
Liquidation Reports and its supporting documents.

10.20. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor supervise the full
implementation of any programs, projects and activities funded by the
National Government in accordance with the timeline and agreements
provided in the MOA.

10.21. The Management promised to monitor the implementation of all projects funded
by the National Government in accordance within the timeframe provided in the
MOA.

11.23. We recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer maximize the utilization
of the Local DRRM Fund by implementing all the programs, projects and
activities in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

11.24. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the
monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th
day after the end of each month, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA
Auditor.

11.25. We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare and
regularly update separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and
Obligations (RAAO) for (a.) QRF, (b.) MOOE and (c.) Capital Outlay for
LDRRM Fund.

11.26. The MDRRM Officer, in coordination of the Municipal Accountant, promised to
comply with the recommendations. The Municipal Budget Officer also promised
to maintain the required budgetary reports.

12.26. We recommended that the MDRRM Officer:
a) include in the LDRRM Investment Plan (LDRRMIP) the PPAs to be
undertaken using the LDDRRM Fund unexpended balances from CY
2021 appropriation and from previous years that were transferred to
Special Trust Fund amounting to ₱1,976,536.70 and ₱6,188,971.23,
respectively (see Appendix D.4);
b) include in the LDRRMIP the program on Stockpiling of Basic
Emergency supplies such as food packs as one of the priorities in the
PPAs.

12.27. We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor and LDRRM
Council immediately provide emergency assistance to victims of calamity or
any disaster in order to save lives, reduce health impacts, ensure public safety
and meet the basic subsistence needs of the people affected.

12.28. The MDRRM Officer, and the LDRRM Council promised to comply with the
recommendations.

13.11. We recommended that Municipal Mayor through its Municipal Development
Council to include in the Annual Investment Plan projects which are
implementation ready to expedite the prosecution of the projects.

13.12. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer to implement all identified
projects within the year to ensure the full utilization of the 20% Development
Fund.

13.13. The Municipal Development Council made an assurance to comply with the
recommendations.

14.21. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor revise the MOA with the
barangays to include the provisions on the specific project to be implemented
and the period within which the project is to be implemented;

14.22. We also recommended that the MPDC should specifically identify the projects
in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) on the financial assistance to the
barangays. Otherwise, if generic term was used, a separate authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan is necessary to authorize the Municipal Mayor to enter
into contract for each financial assistance.

14.23. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. issue demand letters to the barangays concerned for the immediate
submission of liquidation reports and to record the same on the month
these are submitted; and
b. attach to disbursement voucher the certification that the previous
financial assistance was already liquidated and accounted in the books of
accounts.

14.24. The management officials were all amenable with the recommendations.

1.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory
Committee to conduct physical count of all LGU properties, prepare and
submit the required RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant copy furnished the
Audit Team not later than January 31, 2022.
1.6 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain PPE Ledger
Card for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Card for Land;
and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards per category of PPE
to properly monitor the LGU’s properties; and reconcile their respective
records with the RPCPPE; verify any discrepancies noted and effect
corrections to set their records in order.
1.7 We recommended further that the MDRRMO submit copies of the Deed of
Donation and PAR of the communication equipment received from UNDP to
the Accounting Office for recording purposes.

2.9 We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of
inventories, prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant on or before July 31, 2021.
2.10 We also recommended that the Property Officer maintain complete stock
cards and ensure that all inventories are safeguarded and accounted for.
2.11 We further recommended that the concerned personnel in-charge on the
distribution of relief goods and other supplies and materials for distribution
to submit every end of the month the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) with the supporting Requisition and Issue Slips and
Distribution Lists to the accounting unit which will be the basis of the
Municipal Accountant to record the issuances during the month.

3.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor call the attention of the Revenue
Collectors for not remitting their collections daily and require them an
explanation to be submitted to the Audit Team why collections were not
remitted to the Liquidating Officer or Municipal Treasurer daily.
3.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the RCDs daily
and submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of the ensuing month.
For control purposes, he should not issue accountable forms to a Revenue
Collector who has not remitted his collections.
3.14 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the appropriate
accounts in recording prior years’ transactions.

4.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an entry to transfer
the remaining balance of Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs to Trust Liability-
DRRM account to conform with COA Circular 2012-002.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
5.9.1 Restore in the books the cost of inventories totaling ₱87,871.00, if still
on hand, otherwise require from the Municipal Agriculturist the
report for its distribution to end users, and
5.9.2 Record transactions using the correct account codes as prescribed in
the New Chart of Accounts.

6.9 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant submit to the Audit Team justification why such procedure was
adopted instead of preparing disbursement voucher and issuing a check for
each farmer;
6.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification
why payment was made even if the disbursement voucher and supporting
documents were not dated. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice
of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social
Worker to submit the distribution list duly signed by the actual
recipients/beneficiaries and any evidence to support the actual distribution of
rice.

8.17 We recommended that the Municipal Development Council to:
8.17.1 identify and include in the AIP projects which are ready for
implementation in the current budget year to ensure its completion; and
8.17.2 request the assistance of all concerned officials in the preparation of the
budget particularly on cost estimates of PPAs in order to arrive at a
sufficient amount of appropriation.
8.18 Also, we recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to fill-out
completely all the required information in the PO such as the date of the PO
and terms of delivery. Also, Inspection Team indicate date of inspection on AIR.
8.19 Further, we recommended that Inspection Team require their Suppliers or its
representatives to fill-out the date on the Delivery Receipt to facilitate the
computation of liquidated damages in case of late deliveries.

9.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Agriculturist should submit justification for the Audit Team’s evaluation why
liquidated damages was not imposed on the defaulting supplier. This is
without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.
9.10 Moving forward, we recommended that the Inspection Team to indicate in the
Acceptance and Inspection Report the real time of each delivery and to issue
Delivery Receipt of each delivery and not as a whole.
9.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor to avail immediately the
remedies provided in Section 8.4 of RA 9184 should there be unjustified delay
in the delivery of goods and completion of project, which is to rescind the
contract, forfeit the contractor’s performance security and take over the
prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor through
negotiated contract.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification why
these transactions were indorsed for payment despite the deficiencies of the
supporting documents. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Record all lands owned by the LGU based on the report of the Municipal Assessor and all donated assets based on the Deed of Donation; record the donated ambulance unit by inquiring the cost of the same from its Donor or through market research.

2)Compute the Allowance for Accumulated Depreciation on each depreciable asset instead of the aggregate total amount, except the road lot component of the road network system which shall not be subject to depreciation.

3.)We also recommended that all Heads of Offices of the LGU submit a report immediately of all donated properties received by their Office to the General Services Officer and to the Municipal Accountant for recording and monitoring purposes.

observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II No. 1




Reiterated in Part II No. 1

observations.recommendation: 27.) We  recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the refund of the amount from all persons liable thereto.

44.)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish a Local Council for the Protection of Children in the LGU, tasked to develop, identify and implement the programs, projects and activities pursuant to RA 9344 through its Work and Financial Plan (WFP).

45.) We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that funds allotted for the WFP of the LGU’s LCPC is equivalent to 1% of its IRA.

59.) We recommended that the BAC should prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in the prescribed format as required by law. 

60.) Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensures the preparation and submission of the required report.

62.)We also  that the Municipal Accountant should attach a certification that previous cash advances of the employee were already liquidated as part of the supporting documents for purposes of granting cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Inventory Committee to complete, within six months, the conduct of physical count of all LGU properties and prepare the required RPCPPE on or before December 31, 2020 for submission to the Audit Team.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Record all procurement in the Subsidiary Ledgers, reconcile SL balances with the General Ledgers and provide one Property Ledger Card for each PPE account; and
Convene with members of the Road Network Inventory Committee, Property Officer and the Municipal Engineer to resolve the discrepancies between accounting records and the Road Networks Inventory Report.
Further, we recommended that the Designated Property Officer:
Issue Property Acknowlegement Receipt for every equipment issued to an employee to track the custodians of LGU assets. Renew PAR every after  3 years to account for any losses.
Provide a Property Card for each asset of the LGU based on RPCPPE and every time there are newly procured assets; and
Mark assets with Property Tags counted during Physical Inventory.
Management commented that due to lack of personnel, there was a delay in the actual count and reconciliation of records. But the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant are already in the process of reconciling the PPEs based on actual count and per books records and their records of ARE will be renewed to update their property records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Record all donated assets, based on the Deed of Donation and certification of the LGU officials who accepted and has custody of the radio equipment and ambulance;
Review past accounting records to determine which parcels of land are recorded in the books; and tally accounting records with the Inventory given by the Municipal Assessor, so that the value of the Land account in the books of LGU-Caibiran can be presented completely and accurately.
The audit recommendations were already noted by Management and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary entries/adjustment to adjust the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the correct depreciation of all properties under PPE, except Land, every year and submit the computation schedule of the annual depreciation of PPEs to the Audit Team every January of each year.
The Municipal Accountant commented that Journal Entry Vouchers will be prepared to effect the adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Issue a directive to all department heads mandating a centralized receiving of all purchases of the LGU by the GSO/Property Officer only;
Compel the GSO/Property Officer to conduct a physical count of all inventories every June 30 and December 31 each year and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, to confirm the reported balances in the accounting records.
We also recommended that the GSO/Property Officer:
4.11.1 prepare the Requisition and Issue Slip for every supplies issued to be signed by the Head of each requisitioning office;
summarize all RIS in the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of the following month as basis in recording the issuances as deductions in the Inventory account; and
maintain an updated and complete stock cards.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain an updated and complete supplies ledger cards, reconcile accounting records with the RPCI and prepare valid adjustment in case of discrepancies.
The Municipal Mayor had already issued an Executive Order creating an Inventory Committee and ordered the  conduct of physical count thereof. Due to lack of personnel, there was a delay in the actual count and reconciliation of records. The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant are in the process of reconciling their actual and per books records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
issue a directive to the eleven Punong Barangays who received funds from LGU-Caibiran in prior years to submit immediately the Utilization Reports on the said funds to prove that it was used in accordance with public purpose;
stop the grant of additional financial assistance to the Barangays unless the previous release had been fully accounted.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare correcting entries on the funds transferred to Barangays by recording in the Due From Local Government Units account the funds transferred by the LGU to the Barangays, and;
(b) 	inform the Municipal Mayor monthly on unsubmitted Utilization Reports of Barangays as guide in selecting the Barangay qualified for additional grant of funds transfers.
The management commented that demand letters will be sent to the Punong Barangays to submit utilization report on the Financial Assistance received and will grant financial assistance only to those barangays which fully complied with the submission of the required reports.  Also, the Municipal Accountant will prepare Journal Entry vouchers to effect the adjustment this year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all supplies and materials procured by LGU-Caibiran in the appropriate Inventory account and record the expense only when proof of distribution or release to recipients are submitted by the GSO/Property Officer or Head of the requisitioning office.
The Municipal Accountant agreed with the recommendations and commented that Journal Entry Vouchers will be prepared to record the inventories if still undistributed.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: (a) demand from the deputized Barangay Treasurers the remittance of their collections at least once a week, otherwise, the BT should no longer be deputized; (b) keep track of the remittances of deputized collectors; (c) prepare the RCDs daily and submit the said report to the Municipal Accountant every Monday.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) issue a written reminder to the Municipal Treasurer in case of delayed submission of reports, otherwise, inform the Municipal Mayor for appropriate action; and (b) use the appropriate accounts in recording prior years’ transactions.
The recommendations were well noted by management.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate books of accounts for the Municipality’s water system operations and record therein the monthly billings, collections and the related disbursements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the documents necessary to open a separate bank account for the LGU-Caibiran water system operations.
Management did not submit their reply to this observation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement all projects embodied in the Annual Investment Plan so as to utilize fully the 20% Development Fund.
We also recommended that the Local Development Council through the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator include in the Annual Investment Plan Programs, Projects and Activity (PPA) which are attainable with sufficient estimated cost for each PPA.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters to recipients of previous years’ program for the submission of Utilization Reports so that subsequent projects can be implemented.
The recommendations were duly noted by the Management. The Municipal Accountant gave his assurance that demand letters will be sent to recipients of the Economic Program for the Barangays for the submission of Utilization reports and validate its implementation.

We recommended that the MDRRMO shall prepare the LDRRMFIP in the prescribed format, and include a separate caption the programs and activities to be funded from the previous years in the MDRRM-Trust Fund, duly approved by the MDRRM Council and present the 30% QRF in lump-sum.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the “Report on Sources and Utilization” on the LDRRMF monthly and submit the same to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
Management commented that the MDRRM Council convened in 2019 to identify projects and activities to be funded from the 2018 and prior years unexpended LDRRM Trust Fund and it likewise have been adopted and duly approved by the Local Sanggunian on that same year, only that it wasn’t incorporated in the LDRRM Plan. Moreover, recommendations were duly noted by agency and the Municipal Accountant shall hereon promptly submit to COA a monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization on the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Send confirmation letter with the GSIS if there are any unremitted contributions and loan amortizations of LGU employees and remit the balances immediately; and
Remit to the GSIS, within the first ten (10) days of each month, the full amount withheld from employees’ salaries to avoid incurrence of surcharges and enable the employees to avail the benefits which they are entitled to.
The Municipal Accountant explained that the monthly contributions and loan repayments withheld from salaries of all employees for GSIS have already been remitted. The LGU’s actual remittance is based on the Electronic Billing and Collection System of GSIS generated monthly, thus the amount actually remitted and the amount withheld in the payroll may vary.  The unremitted amount identified under Table 12 represents the loan repayments withheld in the payroll but which have been found out to be fully paid or no longer being billed after downloading the collection list for the month under the Electronic Billing  and Collection System. The withheld remittances which didn’t appear in the collection list were then refunded back to the employees in the succeding month.
The Audit Team believes that had refunds been made, the balance of the account would have been zeroed out.  Also, if records of the LGU tallies with that of the GSIS, the amounts deducted in the salaries should be equal to the amount billed by the said institution. There is still an urgent need to update and reconcile LGU records with the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit the full amount of taxes withheld within ten (10) days after the end of each month to avoid penalties and surcharges.
Management asserts that the amounts withheld from employees and suppliers were remitted intact on the succeding month.  Currently, the Accounting office is in the process of reconciling the balance per books.
The records of the LGU contradicts the assertion of management, thus, the Audit Team emphasized the requirement of the BIR to remit within the prescribed dates.

observations.recommendation:
47.)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to ensure full implementation of all priority projects in the AIP in order to maximize the utilization of the fund and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcome for its constituents.

48.)We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for all disbursements and balances of the fund and reconcile immediately the balances from the records of the Budget Officer.

49.) We recommended that the LDRRMO ensure the implementation of all activities in the LDRRMFIP.  

50.) Also, submit the Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Funds on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.

51.)We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) refrain from including in the budget of the SEF items of expenditure other than those enumerated by law.  

52.) Also, the Municipal Mayor and other concerned signing officials stop authorizing payments for expenditures not authorized to be funded from SEF.
53.) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should see to it that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documents to avoid possible suspensions in audit.

54.) We recommended that  the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial Government of Biliran the required quarterly accomplishment report and the Municipal RYF Council shall prepare the updated 6-year updated strategic plan in order that  the objective/vision, mission and major final output of this program was fully  attained.
55.) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should regularly appoint a DRRM Officer as required by law.

56.) We recommended that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat, ensures that all documents required pertaining to procurement be completely attached in the claims made by Contractors. 

57.) Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should be more circumspect in reviewing the supporting documents attached to a claim before processing it.
58.) We recommended that procurement of regular and ordinary supplies and equipment be made in bulk as required by COA rules and regulations

61.) We recommended to require the concerned officials or its equivalent to strictly observe the provisions of Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.
63.) We recommended that the Management should attach a legal document supporting the payment of such benefits.

10.    We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

      a. Record all lands owned by the  LGU  based  on  the  report  of  the
         Municipal Assessor and all donated assets  based  on  the  Deed  of

      b.  Compute  the  Allowance  for  Accumulated  Depreciation  on   each
         depreciable asset instead of the aggregate total amount, except the

11. We also recommended that all Heads of  Offices  of  the  LGU  submit  a
    report immediately of all donated properties received by  their  Office
    to the General Services Officer and to  the  Municipal  Accountant  for
    recording and monitoring purposes.

10. We recommended and the Municipal  Mayor  agreed  to:  (1)  Designate  a
    Property Officer for the LGU, other than the  Municipal  Treasurer,  to
    manage, secure and maintain all properties  of  the  Municipality;  (2)
    Constitute an Inventory Committee who shall conduct a physical count of
    LGU properties and prepare the RPCPPE with complete  information,  and;
    (3) Constitute a Disposal Committee to undertake the  disposal  of  all
    unserviceable properties so that the  same  can  be  removed  from  the
    accounts in the PPE.


11. We also recommended that the designated Property Officer:

         during the conduct of physical count;
      b. Update the Property Acknowledgement Receipt of each employee  every
         three years;
      c. Maintain Property Cards for all properties of the Municipality.

19. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal  Accountant  and
    each Program Coordinator or Focal Person involved in the implementation
    of programs and projects funded by the National Government:


     a. Obtain the MOA and Work and Financial Plans from the Source NGA for
        every fund received;
     b. Furnish the Audit Team with the said  documents  within  five  days
        from receipt thereof;
     c. Implement all programs/projects within the time frame prescribed in
        the MOA to avoid idle funds;
     d. Transfer, within the same month from receipt, the LGSF from General
        Fund bank account to Trust Fund bank account to avoid  the  use  of
        other cash in the Trust Fund;
     e. Return to the source agency the unexpended funds once the  programs
        or projects is completed or if the programs cannot be undertaken by
        the LGU for valid reasons, and;
     f. Submit monthly the  liquidation  reports  within  the  reglementary
        period.

3.20  The personnel concerned stated during the exit  conference  that  they
    will abide with the  recommendations.

7. We recommended that the LDRRMO together with the LDRRM Council  include
    in the annual LDRRMFIP activities to be funded from  the  prior  years’
    Special Trust Fund to avoid audit disallowances and submit  to  COA  at
    the start of the year the annual LDRRMFIP.


8.     We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
     a. Initiate the transfer at year-end of the  unutilized  current  year
        LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund;
     b. Include the balances and utilization from DRRM Trust  Fund  in  the
        Report on Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF;

7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  sign  a  Contract  of  Service
