We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) refrain from including in the budget of the SEF items of expenditure other than those enumerated by law. Also, the Municipal Mayor and other concerned signing officials stop authorizing payments for expenditures not authorized to be funded from SEF.
We recommend that Management should submit the said Report as required by COA regulations.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should regularly appoint a DRRM Officer as required by law.
observations.reason: No qualified applicants for the position

observations.recommendation: We recommend that procurement of regular and ordinary supplies and equipment be made in bulk as required by COA rules and regulations.

We recommend that the BAC prepare the approved PMR and submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in the prescribed format and eventually failed to submit the same to the Government Procurement Policy Board. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the preparation and submission of the required report.

We recommend that Management should stop granting cash advances to an employee if the previous ones are not yet liquidated. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should attach a certification that previous cash advances of the employee were already liquidated as part of the supporting documents for purposes of granting cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a. the Inventory Committee to confirm through inspection the
listed properties per Division, prepare the RPCPPE using the
prescribed format duly approved by the LCE and submit the
said report within the prescribed period to the offices
concerned;
b. the Supply Officer-Designate to maintain Property Cards for
each kind of PPE owned by the Agency and reconcile the same
with the accounting records, and;
c. the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the
prescribed PPELC and periodically reconcile the same with
property records.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry
Vouchers to record the valid book reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation
Statements and revert the stale checks on the month that they occurred or were
detected in order that an accurate and reliable cash balances in the financial
statements are presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council, through the
Local Chief Executive as Chairman, include in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan
development projects only and none for administrative expenses. Further, direct
the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the 20% Development Fund solely
for projects authorized to be paid therefrom to avoid irregular disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor desist from granting
allowances, additional bonuses and such other benefits to LGU officials and
employees without legal basis. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor
initiate the refund of the amount from all persons liable thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Head of HRMO
and the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documentary requirements
provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 including the proof that conditions
cited under DBM Budget Circular No. 2017-3 were complied with. Further,
discontinue the grant of CNA incentives to officials who are not covered by the
afore-cited Budget Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the LDRRM
Fund is utilized solely for PPAs specifically prescribed under NDRRMC-DBM-
DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. Henceforth, purchase of office
supplies, reimbursement for training and travels with purposes other than those
related to disaster prevention, preparedness and mitigation should be paid from the
General Fund of the LGU. Likewise, desist from utilizing the fund for unnecessary
expenditures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all employees to
exercise prudence when filing claims for official travels and observe strictly the
existing provisions of the afore-cited laws, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the
completeness of the supporting documents and ensure the legality, correctness and
reasonableness of the amounts being claimed before payments for travelling expense
are approved. Always require the submission of ORs to support claims for taxi
fares otherwise, no payments should be made for such purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to
send demand letters to the concerned officers and employees of the Municipality
and require them to submit immediately the liquidation documents on their
respective long outstanding accounts. Impose penal sanctions provided for in
COA Circular No. 97-002 should these employees refuse to heed the demand letters.
Further, stop the grant of additional cash advances to employees with
existing unliquidated cash advances

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant submit all financial reports and financial statements within the timeline
prescribed by regulations. Also, submit immediately the lacking disbursement
vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid penal sanctions.
Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose the penal
provisions if the said officials fail to render accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC, Accountant,
Implementing Sections and Supply Officer-Designate to furnish consistently the
Audit Team with the Approved Contracts and Purchase Orders, together with the
required supporting documents, within five (5) days upon perfection of the contract
or issuance of PO, respectively, to preclude audit suspension and/or disallowance of
the transactions.
We further recommended that each project documents be filed in an orderly
manner, contained individually in an expanding envelope, properly labeled
according to the Name of the Project, Project ID, Location, Contractor, Contract
Amount, Source of Fund and Date of Bidding.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to:
1) conduct an inventory of actual occupants of the stalls, require each to
renew their Lease Agreements, determine the amount collectible from
each and demand its immediate payment, otherwise impose civil remedies
to all concerned;
2) make an inventory of all water consumers, issue Demand Letters to all
active accounts, take remedial measures for collection of all delinquent
water consumers and impose the prescribed civil remedies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point of
the LGU to:
a) prepare the Annual GAD Plan and Budget which conforms with the required
threshold of at least five percent (5%) of the total agency budget;
b) prepare and submit a consolidated and fully accomplished GAD
Accomplishment Report using the prescribed form and in accordance with the
reviewed and duly approved GAD Plan and Budget; and
c) submit a certified list of expenditures that can be attributed to the GAD funds
for CY 2017.

We recommended that the SWM Focal Person, Planning Officer, Budget
Officer, Municipal Mayor and all other concerned officials shall:
1. ensure that the LGU’s SWM Plan is marked received and approved by
the NSWMC;
2. prepare the SWM Plan so that the targets embodied therein are those
which are actually and presently needed by the LGU and its constituents;
3. ensure that the SWM Plans and Targets are designed extensively so that
the targets therein are realizable within the year and geared towards an
efficient utilization of the budget for the said program;
4. comply with the requirements of RA 9003 on maintaining a controlled
dumpsite; and
5. provide funds for the Solid Waste Management Program of the
municipality which may be from the 20% Local Development Fund or
collection of fees for provision of solid waste services.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the members of the Local
School Board ensure that programs and activities for Early Childhood Care
Development are incorporated in the budget for SEF and discontinue the inclusion
of appropriations for PPAs which are not authorized under DEPED-DBM-DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s, 2017. Also, review the accuracy on
computations of figures indicated in the budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish a Local Council for
the Protection of Children in the LGU, tasked to develop, identify and implement
the programs, projects and activities pursuant to RA No. 9344 through its Work
and Financial Plan (WFP).
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that funds
allotted for the WFP of the LGU’s LCPC is equivalent to 1% of its IRA.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee to conduct the physical inventory of PPE at least once a year and submit a report on the results thereon. We further recommend that the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) on issued properties be renewed every three (3) years.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to conduct count and reconciliat ion of records

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents immediately.
observations.reason: Some funds are still on the process of posting in the MOA and while some have been posted but still have bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to ensure full implementation of all priority projects in the AIP in order to maximize the utilization of the fund and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcome for its constituents. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for all disbursements and balances of the fund and reconcile immediately the balances from the records of the Budget Officer.

We recommend that the LDRRMO ensure the implementation of all activities in the LDRRMFIP. Also, submit the Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Funds on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.
observations.reason: Findings/ reports not submitted within prescribed period

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board should give equal importance to all priority expenditures enumerated by law in the utilization of SEF by ensuring such would be allocated correspondingly in the preparation of the Annual School Budget.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel/awareness in allocation among priority expenses

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, the Local School Board (LSB), the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer justify and explain why disbursements were made out of SEF appropriations even without an authorized appropriation. Further, the LSB prepare and approve at the start of the year the SEF budget for the ensuing fiscal year.
observations.reason: Findings/ Lack of timely preparation and approval of SEF budget

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and other officials concerned stop the grant of cash advances to employees without the corresponding fidelity bond. Further, the LSB issue a corresponding authority to the person whom they will designate as a special disbursing officer.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a memorandum to all accountable officers requiring them the full liquidation of their respective cash advances. Specifically, require the Municipal Accountant to carry out the following courses of actions: a) For purpose of monitoring, retain a copy of the cash advance voucher and itinerary of travel, as well as keep an index of the cash advances made by each official/employee. b) Issue demand Letters to those who refuse to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. Continued disregard of the demand letters should cause the withholding of any money due the concerned personnel.
observations.reason: Some demand letters sent; monitoring mechanism partly implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documents to avoid possible suspensions in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial Government of Biliran the required quarterly accomplishment report and the Municipal RYF Council shall prepare the updated 6-year strategic plan in order that the objective/vision, mission and major final output of this program was fully attained.

We recommend for management to strictly cause the full liquidation of all pertinent cash advances. For said measure, the Municipal Accountant shall be directed to carry out the hereunder proposed courses of actions; to wit: a. For purposes of monitoring, he shall retain a copy of the cash advance voucher and itinerary of travel, as well as keep an index of the cash advances made by each official/employee. b. He shall send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30-day period to liquidate, a written reminder under signature of the Municipal Mayor, enjoining the official/employee concerned to liquidate his cash advance. c. If the official/employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period, notice to the Auditor concerning the circumstance shall be made for the latter to serve the required demand letter for liquidation as well as explanation for non-liquidation. If after 30 days the liquidation remained not complied with or the explanation given is not satisfactory, the Auditor shall advise for the withholding of any money due the concerned personnel.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, being the LCE, should act with urgency in the mandatory closure of the existing open dumpsite as he may be held liable for violating the solid waste management law.

We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat, ensures that all documents required pertaining to procurement be completely attached in the claims made by Contractors. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant should be more circumspect in reviewing the supporting documents attached to a claim before processing it.

We recommend that Management discontinue the process of paying for procured goods and services through reimbursement. We also recommend that Management should adequately plan its procurement and follow strictly the procedures laid down in RA 9184, as well as its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor, being the Head of the Procuring Entity, should oversee that all procurement laws are followed.

We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat submit the required documents as mandated by COA Regulations. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the immediate submission of the said documents.

We recommend to require the concerned officials or its equivalent to strictly observe the provisions of Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should refrain from making cash advances which are not related to official traveling expenses as such is not authorized under COA regulations. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor should just designate a bonded personnel from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer who will make such cash advances.
We recommend that Management attach a legal document supporting the payment of such benefits.

We recommend that the Local School Board prepare and submit the Approved Special Education Budget. We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer should refrain from certifying existence of available appropriations until such time that the Annual School Budget is approved by the School Board.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, being the LCE, should act with urgency in the mandatory closure of the existing open dumpsite as he may be held liable for violating the solid waste management law.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should regularly appoint a DRRM Officer as required by law.

We recommend that the BAC should prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in the prescribed format as required by law. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the preparation and submission of the required report.

We recommend that Management should stop granting cash advances to an employee if the previous ones are not yet liquidated. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should attach a certification that previous cash advances of the employee were already liquidated as part of the supporting documents for purposes of granting cash advances.

We recommended  that the Municipal Mayor should refrain from making cash advances which are not related to official traveling expenses as such is not authorized under COA regulations.  We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor should just designate a bonded personnel from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer who will make such cash advances.

We recommend that the Local School Board convene and come up yearly with an Approved Special Education Budget. We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer should refrain from certifying existence of available appropriations until such time that the Annual School Budget is approved by the School Board.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor constitute  an Inventory
Committee to conduct the physical count of all PPE of the LGU at least  once
a year and require said Committee  to  submit  the  RPCPPE  on  the  results
thereon.   We  further  recommend  that  the  designated  Property   Officer
maintain Property Cards and  renew  Acknowledgement  Receipt  for  Equipment
(ARE) on issued properties every three (3) years.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  each  program  and
project  coordinator  to  fast-track  the  implementation  of  programs  and
projects funded by the National Government in accordance with  the  timeline
provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the  Liquidation  Reports
and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the  Municipal  Engineer
to ensure full implementation of all priority projects in the AIP  in  order
to maximize the  utilization  of  the  fund  and  achieve  desirable  socio-
economic development and environmental outcome for its constituents.

      We also recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  maintain  Subsidiary
Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for  all  disbursements  and
balances of the  fund  and  reconcile  immediately  the  balances  from  the
records of the Budget Officer.

We  recommend  that  the  LDRRMO  ensure  the  implementation  of  all
activities in the LDRRMFIP.  Also, submit the  Report  on  the  Receipt  and
Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Funds on or before
the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC  and  the  Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local School Board give equal importance to  all
priority  expenditures,  specifically   on   the   improvement   of   school
facilities, in the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget and in  the
utilization of SEF.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, the Local School  Board  (LSB),  the
Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal
Treasurer justify and explain why disbursements were made out  of  SEF  even
without an authorized appropriation.  Further, the LSB prepare  and  approve
at the start of the year the SEF budget for the ensuing fiscal year.

We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) refrain from  including
in the budget of the SEF items of expenditure other  than  those  enumerated
by law.  Also, the Municipal Mayor and  other  concerned  signing  officials
stop authorizing payments for expenditures not authorized to be funded  from
SEF.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and  other  officials  concerned
stop the grant of cash  advances  to  employees  without  the  corresponding
fidelity  bond.   Further,  we  also  recommend  that  the   LSB   issue   a
corresponding authority to the person whom they will designate as a  special
disbursing officer.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  issue  a  Memorandum  to  all
accountable  officers  requiring  them  the  full   liquidation   of   their
respective cash advances.
      Specifically, require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  carry  out  the
following courses of actions:

        a) For purposes of monitoring, retain a copy of  the  cash  advance
           voucher and itinerary of travel, as well as keep an index of the
           cash advances made by each official/employee.

        b) Issue Demand Letters to those who refuse to liquidate their cash
           advances within the prescribed period.  Continued  disregard  of
           the demand letters should cause the withholding of any money due
           the concerned personnel.

We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  ensure  that  all  claims
against government funds are supported  with  complete  documents  to  avoid
possible suspensions in audit.

We  recommend  that   the  Municipal  Accountant  request  from  the
Provincial Government  of  Biliran  the  required  quarterly  accomplishment
report while the Municipal RYF Council prepare the updated 6-year strategic
plan in order that  the objective/vision, mission and major final output  of
this program will be attained.

We recommend for management to strictly cause the full liquidation of all pertinent cash advances. For said measure, the Municipal Accountant shall be directed to carry out the hereunder proposed courses of actions; to wit:
For purposes of monitoring, he shall retain a copy of the cash advance voucher and itinerary of travel, as well as keep an index of the cash advances made by each official/employee.
He shall send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30-day period to liquidate, a written reminder under signature of the Municipal Mayor, enjoining the official/employee concerned to liquidate his cash advance.
If the official/employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period, notice to the Auditor concerning the circumstance shall be made for the latter to serve the required demand letter for liquidation as well as explanation for non-liquidation.
If after 30 days the liquidation remained not complied with or the explanation given is not satisfactory, the Auditor shall advise for the withholding of any money due the concerned personnel.

We recommend that the payment of gross salary and other emoluments of the Municipal Budget Officer be suspended until such time that the required report be submitted and complied therewith as provided for by COA rules and regulations.
We recommend that the APP-CSE should be submitted regularly every November 30 of each year so as to ensure the availability of the projected CSE in the DBM-PS SubDepot.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the Procuring Entity, to refrain from signing the Contract or Memorandum of Agreement with the winning bidder unless the related Performance Security is posted. We also recommend the submission of the related Performance Security on the matter.

We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat, ensures that all documents required pertaining to procurement be completely attached in the claims made by Contractors. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant should be more circumspect in reviewing the supporting documents attached to a claim before processing it.

We recommend that the BAC should ensure that the Abstracts of Canvass or Price Quotation be attached on procurements using the alternative mode of shopping or negotiated procurement as an evidence that they have evaluated the submitted canvass price or price quotation. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant should be vigilant in reviewing the supporting documents attached to a claim before processing it.

We recommend that Management discontinue the process of paying for procured goods and services through reimbursement. We also recommend that Management should adequately plan its procurement and follow strictly the procedures laid down in RA 9184, as well as its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor, being the Head of the Procuring Entity, should oversee that all procurement laws are followed.

We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat submit the required documents as mandated by COA Regulations. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the immediate submission of the said documents.

We recommend to require the concerned officials or its equivalent to strictly observe the provisions of Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with its GAD Focal Point System, should oversee and coordinate with the DILG Regional Office in order to facilitate the approval of the its Annual GPBs. Further, we recommend that the local sangguniang should first obtain the approved GPB before incorporating it and enacting the Annual Budget of the Municipality.
                 Further, we     recommend that the local       sangguniang should first obtain the approved GPB before incorporating it and enacting the Annual Budget of the Municipality.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should ensure timely submission of the LGU’s GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team as required by COA regulations.
We recommend that Management should attach a legal document supporting the payment of such benefits.
